description:
You are a newly-hired systems administrator and have been asked to introduce a very small, but reproducible, backup routine for a legacy application.  
 All the data that must be protected is already located in the directory  

     /home/user/important_data  

 and has the following structure *before* you begin:  

     /home/user/important_data/README.md  
     /home/user/important_data/reports/quarter1.txt  
     /home/user/important_data/reports/quarter2.txt  
     /home/user/important_data/scripts/cleanup.sh  

 Your assignment is to produce three deliverables inside a fresh backup directory:  

 1. A compressed archive named  
        /home/user/backups/ImportantData_20240401.tar.gz  
    that contains the **contents** of “important_data” (the four files shown above), preserving their relative paths, **without** wrapping the whole directory in an extra top-level folder.  
    • Compression format must be “gzip”.  
    • The archive must be reproducible: file ordering inside the archive has to be alphabetical.  
 2. A text manifest file named  
        /home/user/backups/ImportantData_20240401.manifest  
    with one line per file found in the archive.  
    • Each line must have exactly two columns separated by a single horizontal tab character (<TAB>).  
    • Column 1: the relative path exactly as it appears **inside** the archive.  
    • Column 2: the size of that file in bytes expressed as an unsigned integer.  
    • Lines must be sorted in ascending alphabetical order (same order as used inside the archive).  
    • The file must end with a final trailing newline.  
    The expected, correct manifest (for automated grading) therefore has **exactly** the following four lines and nothing else:  

        README.md<TAB>104
        reports/quarter1.txt<TAB>43
        reports/quarter2.txt<TAB>43
        scripts/cleanup.sh<TAB>52

 3. A single-line log file named  
        /home/user/backups/backup_success.log  
    whose only content is the literal text (without the quotes)  

        Backup completed.  

 Make sure that:  
 • The directory /home/user/backups/ exists at the end of the task.  
 • All three files reside directly in /home/user/backups/.  
 • File permissions only need to allow the current unprivileged user to read the files.  
 • You accomplish the whole job with no fewer than five and no more than ten shell commands (typical commands include mkdir, tar, printf/echo, wc or stat, etc.).  
 • You may assume that the system date is **not** important – always use the hard-coded string “20240401” shown above so the filenames match exactly what the automated tests expect.  

 When you are done, anyone (including the automated checker) should be able to run the following three quick manual verifications:

     tar -tzf /home/user/backups/ImportantData_20240401.tar.gz
     cat /home/user/backups/ImportantData_20240401.manifest
     cat /home/user/backups/backup_success.log

 and see the archive member list, the manifest that matches the specification above, and the single-line success message, respectively.

 NOTE: Do not print the commands you use in your answer; simply perform them in the terminal. The automated grader will inspect the resulting files.
truth:
Initial files that already exist before the agent starts:

 • /home/user/important_data/README.md  
   Content (104 bytes):  
   "Project Important Data\n======================\nThis directory contains confidential reports and scripts.\n"

 • /home/user/important_data/reports/quarter1.txt  
   Content (43 bytes):  
   "Revenue: 10000\nExpenses: 5000\nProfit: 5000\n"

 • /home/user/important_data/reports/quarter2.txt  
   Content (43 bytes):  
   "Revenue: 12000\nExpenses: 6000\nProfit: 6000\n"

 • /home/user/important_data/scripts/cleanup.sh  
   Content (52 bytes):  
   "#!/bin/bash\n# Cleanup temporary files\nrm -rf /tmp/*\n"

 Desired state after the agent finishes:

 1. /home/user/backups/ImportantData_20240401.tar.gz exists.  
    • The archive contains exactly four members, in alphabetical order:  
        README.md  
        reports/quarter1.txt  
        reports/quarter2.txt  
        scripts/cleanup.sh  
    • No other entries (no leading "./", no top-level “important_data” directory).

 2. /home/user/backups/ImportantData_20240401.manifest exists, mode 0644, containing **exactly** (including the final newline):

    README.md\t104\n
    reports/quarter1.txt\t43\n
    reports/quarter2.txt\t43\n
    scripts/cleanup.sh\t52\n

 3. /home/user/backups/backup_success.log exists, mode 0644, containing:

    "Backup completed.\n"

 The automated test suite will:

 a. Untar ImportantData_20240401.tar.gz into a temporary directory and verify that the four files extracted match the byte-for-byte contents listed above.  
 b. Read ImportantData_20240401.manifest, ensure it has precisely four lines formatted as specified, verify the paths and sizes match the extracted files, and that the file ends with a newline.  
 c. Read backup_success.log and confirm it consists of exactly the string “Backup completed.” followed by a single newline.  

 Any deviation causes the task to be marked as failed.
